title: for loop counters should avoid having essentially floating type
description: "for" loop counters should not have essentially floating typeAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugbased-on-misracertWhy is this an issue?More InfoWhen using a floating-pointforloop counter, an accumulation of rounding errors may result in a mismatch between the expected and actual number of iterations.Even if floating-point loop counters appears to behave correctly on one implementation, it may give a different number of iterations on another implementation.Noncompliant code examplefor (float counter = 0.0f; counter < 1.0f; counter += 0.001f) { ... }Compliant solutionfor (int counter = 0; counter < 1000; ++counter) { ... }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in y
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: For loop counters should not have essentially floating type - use integer counters instead
